{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"**Triage Order**

1. `src/plugin.ts`  
Refactor first. It has the strongest blast-radius signal among files with an actual refactorable function: `fan-in: 10 importers`, which is above the p95 cutoff `9` and at the p99 cutoff `10`. It also has `buildFinished` at `103 lines`, `1.7x` the 60-line threshold, and `blast_radius: 0.58`. Because many files import it, shrink internals without changing exports first.

2. `src/utils.ts`  
High priority, but more carefully than `plugin.ts`. It has the highest fan-in overall: `18 importers`, far above p95 `9` and p99 `10`, plus a large-file finding: `424 non-empty lines`, `33 top-level functions`, `1.4x` the 300-line threshold. I would not do a broad cleanup pass here. First split clearly isolated utility groups behind compatibility re-exports, because every public helper may be load-bearing.

3. `src/index.ts`  
Refactor early, but after `plugin.ts` / targeted `utils.ts` work. It is both a large file and a major dependency hub: `fan-in 4`, `fan-out 17`, with fan-out above p99 `11`. It also has the worst large-function cluster: `build` `293 lines`, anonymous wrapper `274 lines`, `mainTasks` `194 lines`, `normalizeOptions` `96 lines`, plus large-file evidence of `459 non-empty lines` and `28 top-level functions`. Best first move: extract pure option normalization/build planning helpers before moving side-effecting build/watch orchestration.

4. `src/esbuild/index.ts`  
Next because `runEsbuild` is very large: `237 lines`, `4.0x` threshold, and the file has high fan-out: `11 imports`, exactly p99. But fan-in is only `1`, so fewer callers depend on it than `plugin.ts`, `utils.ts`, or `index.ts`. Refactor by extracting config construction, plugin assembly, timing/logging, and execution paths.

5. `src/esbuild/postcss.ts` and `src/esbuild/svelte.ts`  
Refactor after the central build path. `postcss.ts` has high large-function risk: `postcssPlugin` `140 lines`, `setup` `126 lines`, both with test gap. `svelte.ts` is slightly lower: `sveltePlugin` `116 lines`, `setup` `106 lines`, anonymous function `91 lines`. Neither appears in the high fan-in/fan-out set, so these are contained plugin-level cleanups.

6. `src/api-extractor.ts`, `src/tsc.ts`, `src/rollup.ts`, `src/rollup/ts-resolve.ts`  
Lower priority. They have medium large-function findings, but less central connectivity. `api-extractor.ts` has `fan-out: 6`, exactly p95, and `rollupDtsFiles` is `85 lines`. `rollup.ts` has `fan-out: 7`, but `blast_radius: 0`; `getRollupConfig` is `122 lines`. `tsc.ts` has `emitDtsFiles` at `73 lines`. These are worth incremental extraction, not first-wave architecture work.

**Leave Alone For Now**

`src/log.ts` and `test/utils.ts` should be treated as load-bearing but not refactored first. `src/log.ts` has `fan-in: 9 importers`, and `test/utils.ts` also has `fan-in: 9 importers`, both at the p95 cutoff. However, neither has large-file or large-function findings in this scan. Changing their public surface would create risk without obvious complexity reduction.

I would also leave `src/esbuild/swc.ts`, `src/plugins/swc-target.ts`, and `src/plugins/terser.ts` until later. Their functions are just over threshold: `65`, `62`, and `62` lines. Those are cleanup candidates, not blast-radius reducers."}}
